The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 410d8849).

## Issue to Resolve

Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Autoformatting should not reformat below line to incorrect state
```
private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Observed Behavior
Autoformatting moves `DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))` to the next line and as the results it looks like this:
```
    private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = 
    DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Autoformatting should allow to reproduce this. My Detekt config:
```
formatting:
    excludes: [ '**/build/**' ]
    MaximumLineLength:
        active: false
    NoBlankLineBeforeRbrace:
        active: false
    Indentation:
        active: false
    ArgumentListWrapping:
        active: false
    MultiLineIfElse:
        active: false
```

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.49.1
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used: Detekt 1.23.0. Related issue https://github.com/detekt/detekt/issues/6223
* Version of Gradle: 7.3.3
* Kotlin version 1.8.21

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyWrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:

**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a property.
 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,
139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {
142→        emit(
143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
154→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
155→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
156→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
157→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
158→    ) {
159→        emit(
160→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
161→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
162→            true,
163→        )
164→        LOGGER.trace {
165→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {
168→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
169→        }
170→    }
171→}
172→
173→public val PROPERTY_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyWrappingRule().ruleId

content.thinking:
**Analyzing indent issue with wrapped properties**

The property-wrapping rule may still wrap lines based on a default max line length even if the MaximumLineLength rule is disabled, causing incorrectly indented wrapped values; next steps include confirming how the max line length is handled when the rule is inactive and investigating how indentation is applied in those wrapped lines.**Investigating incorrect indentation after equals**

Inserted newlines after the '=' sign should receive the child indent of the '=' node, but current output shows no added indent beyond the property line; I'll need to inspect how childIndentOf computes indentation for the '=' node and why it results in insufficient indent.

 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties

 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,

content.thinking:
**Analyzing indent calculation discrepancy**

Observed that childIndentOf yields the same indent as the property line instead of adding an extra indent, likely because the indentation config is disabled or empty, causing indent step to be zero and preventing additional indentation on child lines.**Identifying wrapping logic conflict with disabled indentation**

Found that disabling indentation and maximum line length rules likely sets their limits to zero, causing unconditional wrapping with no indent increase; the property wrapping rule should skip wrapping when max line length is disabled to prevent unwanted formatting changes.

 43→    StandardRule(
 44→        id = "parameter-wrapping",
 45→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 46→            setOf(
 47→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 48→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 49→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 50→            ),

 54→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        line = 1
 58→        indentConfig =
 59→            IndentConfig(
 60→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 61→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 62→            )
 63→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER) {
 72→            rearrangeValueParameter(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun rearrangeValueParameter(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        require(node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 82→
 83→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 84→
 85→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 86→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 87→        val fromNode =
 88→            node
 89→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 90→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 91→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 92→                ?: node
 93→
 94→        node
 95→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 96→            ?.let { colon ->
 97→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 98→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 99→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
100→                    return
101→                }
102→            }
103→
104→        node
105→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
106→            ?.let { typeReference ->
107→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
108→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                    return
110→                }
111→            }
112→
113→        node
114→            .findChildByType(EQ)
115→            ?.let { equal ->
116→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
117→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
118→                    return
119→                }
120→            }
121→
122→        node
123→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
124→            ?.let { callExpression ->
125→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
126→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
127→                    return
128→                }
129→            }
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.orTrailingComma() =
133→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
134→            .nextCodeLeaf()
135→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == COMMA }
136→            ?: this
137→
138→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
139→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
140→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
141→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
142→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
143→    }

145→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(

201→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
202→        node: ASTNode,
203→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
204→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
205→    ) {
206→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
207→        var newlineInBetween = false
208→        var parameterListInBetween = false
209→        var numberOfArgs = 0
210→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
211→        // matching ), ] or }
212→        val r = node.nextSibling {
213→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
214→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()

216→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST

218→                numberOfArgs++
219→                firstArg = it
220→            }
221→            it.elementType == rElementType
222→        }!!
223→        if (
224→            !newlineInBetween ||
225→            // keep { p ->
226→            // }
227→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
228→            // keep ({
229→            // }) and (object : C {
230→            // })
231→            (
232→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
233→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
234→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
235→                )
236→        ) {
237→            return
238→        }
239→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
240→            // keep:
241→            // for (foo in listOf(
242→            //     "foo-1",
243→            //     "foo-2"
244→            // )) { ... }
245→            // but reject:
246→            // for (
247→            //     foo in listOf(
248→            //         "foo-1",
249→            //         "foo-2"
250→            //     )
251→            // ) { ... }
252→            return
253→        }
254→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
255→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
256→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
257→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
258→                    it.textLength > 0
259→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
260→            // IDEA quirk:
261→            // if (true &&
262→            //     true
263→            // ) {
264→            // }
265→            // instead of
266→            // if (
267→            //     true &&
268→            //     true
269→            // ) {
270→            // }
271→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
272→        ) {
273→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)

468→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470→        // if token is in lTokenSet
471→        //     find matching rToken
472→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473→        // return false
474→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477→            // special cases:
478→            // x = y.f({ z ->
479→            // })
480→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481→            // 2, 3)
482→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483→        }
484→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }

700→                if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {

702→                    node

713→                    // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
714→                    node
715→                } else {
716→                    // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
717→                    node.nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }

**Normalizing invalid max line length**

The property wrapping rule lacks a check for maxLineLength being OFF, causing unexpected wrapping when maxLineLength is 0 or invalid. Normalizing maxLineLength ≤ 0 to OFF before applying the rule should fix this.**Planning minimal maxLineLength fix in property rule**

Deciding to normalize maxLineLength ≤ 0 to OFF within PropertyWrappingRule’s beforeFirstNode for a targeted fix, avoiding wider changes. This ensures disabled or invalid line length disables wrapping cleanly without affecting other rules.

 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,
139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {
142→        emit(
143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
154→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
155→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
156→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
157→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
158→    ) {
159→        emit(
160→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
161→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
162→            true,
163→        )
164→        LOGGER.trace {
165→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {
168→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
169→        }
170→    }
171→}
172→
173→public val PROPERTY_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyWrappingRule().ruleId

 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}

118→                    return

120→            }
121→
122→        node
123→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
124→            ?.let { callExpression ->
125→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
126→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
127→                    return
128→                }
129→            }
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.orTrailingComma() =
133→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
134→            .nextCodeLeaf()
135→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == COMMA }
136→            ?: this
137→
138→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
139→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
140→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
141→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
142→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
143→    }
144→
145→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
146→        node: ASTNode,
147→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
148→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149→    ) {
150→        emit(
151→            node.startOffset - 1,
152→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
153→            true,
154→        )
155→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
156→        if (autoCorrect) {
157→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
162→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
163→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
164→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
165→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
166→    ) {
167→        emit(
168→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
169→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
170→            true,
171→        )
172→        LOGGER.trace {
173→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
174→        }
175→        if (autoCorrect) {
176→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
177→        }
178→    }
179→}

181→public val PARAMETER_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ParameterWrappingRule().ruleId

 90→/**
 91→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 92→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 93→ *
 94→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 95→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 96→ * indentation rule is not run.
 97→ */
 98→public class WrappingRule :
 99→    StandardRule(
100→        id = "wrapping",
101→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
102→            setOf(
103→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
104→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
105→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
106→            ),
107→        visitorModifiers =
108→            setOf(
109→                RunAfterRule(
110→                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
111→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
112→                ),
113→            ),
114→    ) {
115→    private var line = 1
116→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
117→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
118→
119→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
120→        line = 1
121→        indentConfig =
122→            IndentConfig(
123→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
124→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
125→            )
126→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
127→    }
128→
129→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
130→        node: ASTNode,
131→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
132→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
133→    ) {
134→        when (node.elementType) {
135→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
138→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
140→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
141→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
142→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
143→            SEMICOLON -> insertNewLineAfterSemi(node, autoCorrect, emit)
144→        }
145→    }

147→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
148→        node: ASTNode,
149→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
151→    ) {
152→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
153→
154→        val lbrace =
155→            node
156→                .getStartOfBlock()
157→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
158→                ?: return
159→        if (lbrace.followedByNewline() ||
160→            lbrace.followedByEolComment() ||
161→            lbrace.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY)
162→        ) {
163→            // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
164→            return
165→        }
166→
167→        node
168→            .takeUnless { it.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
169→            ?.getEndOfBlock()
170→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
171→            ?.let { rbrace ->
172→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
173→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
174→                }
175→            }
176→
177→        if (maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
178→            val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
179→                node
180→                    .leaves(false)
181→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
182→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
183→            val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
184→                node
185→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
186→                    .leavesIncludingSelf()
187→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
188→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
189→            if (lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength) {
190→                requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
191→            }
192→        }
193→    }
194→
195→    private fun ASTNode.followedByEolComment() =
196→        null !=
197→            leaves()
198→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
199→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
200→
201→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
202→        node: ASTNode,
203→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
204→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
205→    ) {
206→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
207→        var newlineInBetween = false
208→        var parameterListInBetween = false
209→        var numberOfArgs = 0
210→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
211→        // matching ), ] or }
212→        val r = node.nextSibling {
213→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
214→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
215→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
216→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
217→            if (isValueArgument) {
218→                numberOfArgs++
219→                firstArg = it
220→            }
221→            it.elementType == rElementType
222→        }!!
223→        if (
224→            !newlineInBetween ||
225→            // keep { p ->
226→            // }
227→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
228→            // keep ({
229→            // }) and (object : C {
230→            // })
231→            (
232→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
233→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
234→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
235→                )
236→        ) {
237→            return
238→        }
239→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
240→            // keep:
241→            // for (foo in listOf(
242→            //     "foo-1",
243→            //     "foo-2"
244→            // )) { ... }
245→            // but reject:
246→            // for (
247→            //     foo in listOf(
248→            //         "foo-1",
249→            //         "foo-2"
250→            //     )
251→            // ) { ... }
252→            return
253→        }
254→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
255→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
256→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
257→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
258→                    it.textLength > 0
259→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
260→            // IDEA quirk:
261→            // if (true &&
262→            //     true
263→            // ) {
264→            // }
265→            // instead of
266→            // if (
267→            //     true &&
268→            //     true
269→            // ) {
270→            // }
271→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
272→        ) {
273→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
274→        }
275→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
276→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
277→        }
278→    }

280→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
281→        node: ASTNode,
282→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
283→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
284→    ) {
285→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
286→        if (
287→            node.textContains('\n') &&
288→            entries.size > 1 &&
289→            // e.g.
290→            //
291→            // class A : B, C,
292→            //     D
293→            // or
294→            // class A : B, C({
295→            // }), D
296→            //
297→            // but not
298→            //
299→            // class A : B, C, D({
300→            // })
301→            !(
302→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
303→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
304→                )
305→        ) {
306→            // put space after :
307→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
308→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
309→                if (
310→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
311→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
312→                ) {
313→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
314→                }
315→            }
316→            // put entries on separate lines
317→            for (c in node.children()) {
318→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
319→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
320→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
321→                ) {
322→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
323→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
324→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
325→                        emit = emit,
326→                        indent = node.indent(),
327→                    )
328→                }
329→            }
330→        }
331→    }
332→
333→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
334→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
335→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
336→
337→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
338→        node: ASTNode,
339→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
340→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
341→    ) {
342→        for (c in node.children()) {
343→            val hasLineBreak =
344→                when (c.elementType) {
345→                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
346→                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
347→                    else -> false
348→                }
349→            if (hasLineBreak) {
350→                // rearrange
351→                //
352→                // a, b, value(
353→                // ), c, d
354→                //
355→                // to
356→                //
357→                // a, b,
358→                // value(
359→                // ),
360→                // c, d
361→
362→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
363→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
364→                if (
365→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
366→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
367→                ) {
368→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
369→                }
370→                // insert \n after multi-line value
371→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
372→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
373→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
374→                if (
375→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
376→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
377→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
378→                    // value(
379→                    // ), // a comment
380→                    // c, d
381→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
382→                ) {
383→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
384→                }
385→            }
386→        }
387→    }
388→
389→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
390→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
391→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
392→
393→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
394→        node: ASTNode,
395→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
396→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
397→    ) {
398→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
399→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
400→            node
401→                .children()
402→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
403→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
404→                    typeProjection
405→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
406→                        .let { prevSibling ->
407→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
408→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
409→                                if (autoCorrect) {
410→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
411→                                }
412→                            }
413→                        }
414→                }
415→
416→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
417→            node
418→                .findChildByType(GT)
419→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
420→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
421→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
422→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
423→                        if (autoCorrect) {
424→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
425→                        }
426→                    }
427→                }
428→
429→            Unit
430→        }
431→    }

433→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434→        node: ASTNode,
435→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437→    ) {
438→        node
439→            .treeParent
440→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445→            ?.let {
446→                // rewriting
447→                // """
448→                //     text
449→                // _""".trimIndent()
450→                // to
451→                // """
452→                //     text
453→                // _
454→                // """.trimIndent()
455→                emit(
456→                    node.startOffset,
457→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",

461→                    node as LeafPsiElement

468→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470→        // if token is in lTokenSet
471→        //     find matching rToken
472→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473→        // return false
474→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477→            // special cases:
478→            // x = y.f({ z ->
479→            // })
480→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481→            // 2, 3)
482→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483→        }
484→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
487→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
488→        }
489→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
490→            return true
491→        }
492→        return false
493→    }

495→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
496→        node: ASTNode,
497→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
498→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
499→    ) {
500→        val p = node.treeParent
501→        if (
502→            // check
503→            // `{ p -> ... }`
504→            // and
505→            // `when { m -> ... }`
506→            // only
507→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
508→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
509→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
510→            // permit
511→            // when {
512→            //     m -> 0 + d({
513→            //     })
514→            // }
515→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
516→            // permit
517→            // when (this) {
518→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
519→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
520→            //     -> true
521→            // }
522→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
523→        ) {
524→            return
525→        }
526→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
527→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
528→        }
529→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
530→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
531→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
532→        }
533→    }

535→    private fun insertNewLineAfterSemi(
536→        node: ASTNode,
537→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
538→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
539→    ) {
540→        val previousCodeLeaf = node.prevCodeLeaf()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
541→        val nextCodeLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
542→        if (previousCodeLeaf.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY && nextCodeLeaf.elementType == RBRACE) {
543→            // Allow
544→            // enum class INDEX2 { ONE, TWO, THREE; }
545→            return
546→        }
547→        if (noNewLineInClosedRange(previousCodeLeaf, nextCodeLeaf)) {
548→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit, previousCodeLeaf.indent())
549→            node
550→                .treeParent
551→                .takeIf { it.elementType == BLOCK }
552→                ?.let { block ->
553→                    beforeVisitBlock(block, autoCorrect, emit)
554→                    block
555→                        .treeParent
556→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
557→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
558→                        ?.let { arrow -> rearrangeArrow(arrow, autoCorrect, emit) }
559→                }
560→        }
561→    }

563→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
564→        node: ASTNode,
565→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
566→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
567→        indent: String,
568→    ) {
569→        emit(
570→            node.startOffset - 1,

572→            true,
573→        )
574→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }

580→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
581→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
582→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
583→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
584→        indent: String? = null,
585→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
586→    ) {
587→        emit(
588→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
589→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
590→            true,
591→        )
592→        LOGGER.trace {

594→        }
595→        if (autoCorrect) {

601→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
602→        for (child in node.children()) {
603→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
604→                val v = child.text
605→                if (v == "\n") {
606→                    return true
607→                }
608→            }
609→        }
610→        return false
611→    }

613→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
614→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
615→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
616→            textContains('\n')
617→        } else {
618→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
619→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
620→        }

632→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
633→     *     for (foo in listOf(
634→     *         "foo-1",
635→     *         "foo-2"
636→     *     )) { ... }
637→     * but reject:

645→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
646→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
647→            return false
648→        }
649→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
650→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
651→        }
652→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
653→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
654→        }
655→
656→        var node: ASTNode? = this
657→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
658→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
659→                return false
660→            }
661→            node = node.nextSibling()
662→        }
663→        return true
664→    }

666→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
667→        node: ASTNode,
668→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
669→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
670→    ) {
671→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
672→            val lbrace =
673→                node
674→                    .getStartOfBlock()
675→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
676→                    ?: return
677→            node
678→                .getEndOfBlock()
679→                ?.takeUnless { it.isPrecededByNewline() }
680→                ?.let { rbrace ->
681→                    if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
682→                        requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
683→                            rbrace,
684→                            autoCorrect,
685→                            emit,
686→                            indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node),
687→                        )
688→                    }
689→                }
690→        }
691→    }

693→    private fun ASTNode.followedByNewline() = nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()

695→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByNewline() = prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()

697→    private fun ASTNode.getStartOfBlock() =
698→        firstChildLeafOrSelf()
699→            .let { node ->
700→                if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
701→                    // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
702→                    node
703→                } else {
704→                    // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
705→                    node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
706→                }
707→            }
708→
709→    private fun ASTNode.getEndOfBlock() =
710→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
711→            .let { node ->
712→                if (node.elementType == RBRACE && treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
713→                    // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
714→                    node
715→                } else {
716→                    // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
717→                    node.nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
718→                }
719→            }

724→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
725→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
726→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
